priceNote: The 500.00 Birr pass card membership fee will be donated to the Samrawit Foundation Charitable Endowment.

programs.name: Strength Track
programs.desc: Barbell progressions with weekly performance tracking and coaching feedback.

programs.name: Hybrid Conditioning
programs.desc: Engine-building sessions that mix cardio, sled work, and functional strength.

programs.name: Recovery Flow
programs.desc: Mobility classes, breathwork, and guided stretching for longevity.

programs.name: Athlete Performance
programs.desc: Power, speed, and agility for sport-specific results.

coaches.name: Kidus Mulgeta
coaches.bio: 10+ years coaching strength athletes and general population clients.
coaches.exp: Focus: Maximum strength, clean technique, injury-proof mechanics.

coaches.name: Eskinder Worku
coaches.bio: Hybrid endurance specialist with a focus on sustainable intensity.
coaches.exp: Focus: Engine building, athletic conditioning, interval systems.

coaches.name: Niah Hassen
coaches.bio: Movement coach helping members improve range and reduce pain.
coaches.exp: Focus: Mobility systems, tissue care, pre-hab and post-hab.

machines.name: Multi-Station Cable Machine
machines.desc: Functional trainer with adjustable pulleys for upper body, core, and full-body cable work.
machines.tip: Set both pulleys evenly and stay controlled through the full range.

machines.name: Seated Chest Press Machine
machines.desc: Guided chest press machine that lets members load the chest, triceps, and front delts safely.
machines.tip: Keep your shoulders down and drive straight through the handles.

machines.name: Pec Deck / Chest Fly Machine
machines.desc: Chest isolation station built for controlled inward pressing and fly movement.
machines.tip: Keep a soft bend in the elbows and squeeze at the center.

machines.name: Seated Calf Raise
machines.desc: Lower-leg machine designed to load the calves in a seated position for controlled plantar flexion work.
machines.bestFor: Calf strength, ankle support, lower-leg development
machines.tip: Pause at the top, lower slowly, and move through the full ankle range.

machines.name: Inclined Leg Press Machine
machines.desc: Angled sled machine for heavy lower-body pressing with stable foot placement.
machines.bestFor: Leg strength and high-volume lower-body work
machines.tip: Drive through the mid-foot and avoid locking the knees hard.

machines.name: Smith Machine
machines.desc: Barbell-on-rails setup for safer pressing and squat patterns with guided movement.
machines.tip: Set the safeties before loading the bar and keep your path stacked.

machines.name: Adjustable Benches
machines.desc: Flat and incline benches used throughout the free-weight area for pressing and dumbbell work.
machines.tip: Lock the bench angle first so the setup stays solid through the set.

machines.name: Abductors Machine
machines.desc: Seated lower-body machine designed to strengthen the outer hips through controlled abduction.
machines.targets: Glute medius, outer hips, hip stabilizers
machines.bestFor: Hip strength, lower-body balance, glute activation
machines.tip: Stay tall in the seat and press outward with control instead of jerking the weight.

machines.name: Dumbbell Rack
machines.desc: Full rack of dumbbells for progressive free-weight training across all major movement patterns.
machines.tip: Choose the pair you can control cleanly for every rep.

machines.name: Leg Extension Machine
machines.desc: Seated lower-body machine designed to isolate the quadriceps through controlled knee extension.
machines.bestFor: Quad isolation, knee strength, lower-body development
machines.tip: Extend smoothly, pause at the top, and lower the weight under control.

machines.name: Treadmills
machines.desc: Multiple treadmills positioned near the windows for walking, runs, and conditioning blocks.
machines.tip: Start with a steady pace before adding speed or incline.

machines.name: Elliptical Machines
machines.desc: Low-impact cross-trainers for steady cardio and recovery-oriented conditioning.
machines.tip: Keep the motion smooth and drive evenly through both pedals.

machines.name: Stair Climber
machines.desc: Stepper-style cardio machine for intense leg-focused conditioning.
machines.tip: Stay upright and let the legs do the work instead of leaning on the rails.

machines.name: Punching Bag
machines.desc: Heavy bag station for boxing drills, conditioning rounds, and athletic movement work.
machines.tip: Brace your core and rotate through the hips, not just the arms.

machines.name: Spin Bikes
machines.desc: Indoor cycling bikes used for cardio blocks, endurance rides, and class-style sessions.
machines.tip: Set the saddle height first so the knees stay comfortable through the ride.

machines.name: Preacher Curl Machine
machines.desc: Arm-focused machine built to isolate the biceps with a supported upper-arm position and strict curl path.
machines.bestFor: Biceps isolation, strict curls, arm development
machines.tip: Keep your upper arms planted on the pad and control the lowering phase.

machines.name: Mats / Functional Space
machines.desc: Open floor area for stretching, mobility drills, bodyweight work, and general movement prep.
machines.tip: Use the space for prep work before loading the main lifts.
